Document:
# Medical V3
## Overview - Marines now suffer wounds, bleeding, fractures, organ damage, embedded shrapnel, severance, and surgery state on specific body regions. - Field treatment only works on the selected body region. If the selected region cannot be helped, the body selector opens so you can choose a useful target. - Treated wounds still leave damage behind. Corpsmen stabilize the patient in the field; doctors and surgeons finish internal bleeding, organs, fractures, shrapnel, and limb work. - Medical crafting for packed gauze, coagulant powder, burn gel, and plain trauma dressings has been removed.
## Field Treatments
## Surgical line and synth graft - Surgical line is a temporary brute bleed suppression tool for a chosen body region. - Synth graft is a temporary burn suppression tool for a chosen body region. - Both stop the field problem long enough to move the patient, but they do not replace proper wound treatment or surgery. - Internal bleeding, fractures, organs, shrapnel, and severed limbs still need the correct surgical procedure.
